Being married to a man isn't illegal, and is, in fact, quite common. Only problem is that once you do marry a man, you are no longer able to inherit any titles. So it is mainly seen as a punishment.

Zheng was a mute from an upper-middle class family. His only redeeming quality was that he was pretty. He didn't even have a name until he was 15.

☆

AKA: you were forced to marry him as the Prince to the throne.

TW: factually INCORRECT statements about China (based on ancient China LOOSELY), I can't stress this enough. Forced marriage, non-con/sub-con.
